title: Stackable Wood Cake Stand
summary: Two open-frame cake stands, one smaller and one larger, built to stack directly on top of each other for a two-tier display — each stand is just a simple 1x4 box frame.
description:
Each stand is the same simple build at a different scale: a rectangular 1x4 frame made from two end pieces and a set of parallel slats spanning between them, glued and nailed together flat. **Build the smaller stand and the larger stand as separate, complete assemblies** — they don't share any parts, and the smaller one is sized to sit centered on top of the larger one once both are finished.

Space the slats evenly across each frame as you nail them down — consistent gaps are what make this read as a considered stand rather than a scrap-wood tray. Stain both stands the same color so the stack reads as one matched two-tier piece rather than two unrelated boxes.

Once both stands are built and finished, simply set the smaller one centered on top of the larger one — nothing fastens them together, so the top stand lifts off cleanly when you need to serve straight off the tier below.

cutList.note: 2 outer frame slats plus 3 inner slats.
2 outer frame slats plus 5 inner slats.

steps.title: Build the smaller stand
steps.body: Assemble the small stand's frame ends and slats flat, gluing and nailing with even gaps between slats.

steps.title: Build the larger stand
steps.body: Assemble the large stand's frame ends and slats the same way, at its larger size.

steps.title: Finish and stack
steps.body: Sand and stain both stands the same color, then set the smaller stand centered on top of the larger one.
